Title: Isolation of amaranthin synthetase from Chenopodium quinoa and construction of an amaranthin production system using suspension-cultured tobacco BY-2 cells

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: betacyanin modification branch; amaranthin formation from betanin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucuronic acid to betanin to form amaranthin with a β-1,2-glycosidic linkage on the glucoside moiety.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables amaranthin production; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and stable expression in tobacco BY-2 cells led to amaranthin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in quinoa hypocotyl; CqAmaSy1b transcript detected, CqAmaSy1a not detected at transcript level by 3'-UTR polymorphism assay.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in agroinfiltrated N. benthamiana leaves and transgenic BY-2 cells by RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant pigment with reported anti-inflammatory, anticancer, and HIV-1 protease inhibitory activities.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: quinoa hypocotyl; engineered tobacco BY-2 cells; transiently infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 23°C growth for quinoa plants; transient assay in N. benthamiana leaves; constitutive transgene expression in BY-2 suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic candidate selection; RT-PCR expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; HPLC and LC-MS metabolite identification; stable transformation of tobacco BY-2 cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqAmaSy1 was identified from quinoa as an amaranthin synthetase candidate expressed in hypocotyl. 2. Co-expression with CqCYP76AD1-1, CqDODA-1 and CqCDOPA5GT produced amaranthin in N. benthamiana leaves. 3. Substrate testing with DbB5GT showed that CqAmaSy1 uses betanin as substrate. 4. Introduction into BY-2 cells with three upstream betalain genes enabled amaranthin production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of non-betalain plants or cultured cells to produce amaranthin on demand; can be stacked with upstream betalain genes for pharmaceutical or nutraceutical production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In BY-2 constitutive expression, both betanin and amaranthin accumulated, suggesting incomplete flux conversion to amaranthin; no major growth penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CqAmaSy1 corresponds functionally to at least CqAmaSy1b expressed in hypocotyl; CqAmaSy1a and CqAmaSy1b share the same ORF and are adjacent in the quinoa genome.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: betacyanin modification branch; amaranthin formation from betanin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Encodes an amaranthin synthetase activity that converts betanin to amaranthin in the heterologous transient system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports amaranthin production in transient Nicotiana benthamiana assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves by transient expression.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by HPLC metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqAmaSy2 was identified as a homolog of CqAmaSy1 in quinoa. 2. Co-expression with upstream quinoa betalain genes yielded amaranthin in N. benthamiana leaves. 3. Unlike CqAmaSy1b, CqAmaSy2 was not expressed in quinoa hypocotyl.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative amaranthin synthetase for heterologous amaranthin production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native contribution to hypocotyl amaranthin accumulation appears unlikely due to lack of expression in hypocotyl.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study concludes at least three quinoa genes encode amaranthin synthetase activity: CqAmaSy1a, CqAmaSy1b and CqAmaSy2.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes amaranthin formation in transient expression assays, likely by glucuronylation of betanin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced amaranthin when expressed with upstream quinoa betalain genes in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves by transient expression context.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by HPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bv3GGT-like1 is a beet ortholog-like gene related to CqAmaSy. 2. Its expression in N. benthamiana produced both amaranthin and betanin, demonstrating amaranthin synthetase activity. 3. The paper suggests such enzymes may not function in beet hypocotyl despite their presence in the genome.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for engineering amaranthin production from beet-family genes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Generated both amaranthin and residual betanin, indicating incomplete conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests beet hypocotyl accumulates betanin only, implying limited native function or expression of Bv3GGT-like1 in that organ.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes amaranthin formation in transient expression assays, likely via glucuronylation of betanin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced amaranthin along with betanin in transient Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves by transient expression context.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by HPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AhUGT79B30-like3 from amaranth showed amaranthin synthetase activity in N. benthamiana. 2. Leaves expressing this gene accumulated both amaranthin and betanin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative amaranth-derived amaranthin synthetase candidate for heterologous production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Residual betanin indicates incomplete conversion to amaranthin.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less specific than AhUGT79B30-like4 in the transient assay.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of betanin to amaranthin in transient expression assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produced amaranthin as the only detected major product in transient Nicotiana benthamiana leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves by transient expression context.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: transiently infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves; amaranth hypocotyl inferred in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by HPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AhUGT79B30-like4, the closest orthologue of CqAmaSy, showed strong amaranthin synthetase activity. 2. In transient assays, leaves accumulated only amaranthin, indicating efficient conversion from betanin. 3. Discussion identifies it as a likely enzyme for amaranthin biosynthesis in amaranth hypocotyl.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising enzyme for efficient amaranthin bioproduction due to high product specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most specific amaranthin-producing ortholog tested among beet/amaranth candidates.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Core upstream betalain biosynthesis enzyme required together with DODA and glycosyltransferase genes to generate betanin and amaranthin in heterologous systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for reconstruction of betanin and amaranthin biosynthesis in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves and tobacco BY-2 cells.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in quinoa hypocotyl; used as positive control in RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated leaves and transgenic BY-2 cells by RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: quinoa hypocotyl; engineered BY-2 cells; infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient and stable heterologous expression systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction by transient co-expression and stable transformation with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqCYP76AD1-1 is one of the core quinoa betalain biosynthesis genes expressed in hypocotyl. 2. It was necessary for betanin and amaranthin reconstitution in heterologous tobacco systems.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential upstream module for engineering betalain production when stacked with DODA and glycosyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major tradeoff reported; betanidin-only engineering yielded little stable pigment accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific reaction details were not experimentally dissected in this paper, but gene function was used based on prior study and pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of betalain precursor betalamic acid from L-DOPA in the reconstructed pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for reconstruction of betanin and amaranthin production in heterologous tobacco systems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated leaves and transgenic BY-2 cells by RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: quinoa hypocotyl; engineered BY-2 cells; infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient and stable heterologous expression systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction by transient co-expression and stable transformation with metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqDODA-1 is a core quinoa betalain biosynthesis gene expressed in hypocotyl. 2. It was required for the engineered production of betanin and amaranthin in tobacco systems.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential upstream precursor-supplying gene for betalain engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in all successful heterologous pathway reconstructions in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: core betacyanin formation leading to betanin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates cyclo-DOPA to cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside and in the heterologous system supports synthesis of betanin and cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for betanin production and thereby for downstream amaranthin synthesis in heterologous assays and BY-2 cells.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated leaves and transgenic BY-2 cells by RT-PCR.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: quinoa hypocotyl; engineered BY-2 cells; infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient and stable heterologous expression systems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway reconstruction by transient co-expression and stable transformation with metabolite profiling; substrate-discrimination experiment using DbB5GT as comparator
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CqCDOPA5GT is expressed in quinoa hypocotyl and is one of the core genes for betalain biosynthesis. 2. Co-expression with CqCYP76AD1-1 and CqDODA-1 enabled betanin production. 3. Its inclusion with CqAmaSy1 enabled amaranthin formation, although substrate testing showed CqAmaSy1 uses betanin rather than cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Necessary component for engineering betanin/amaranthin production in non-betalain hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Because it produces both betanin and cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside in the transient system, it complicates substrate assignment for downstream enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper discusses possible native route in quinoa: cyclo-DOPA 5-O-glucoside to betanin, then betanin to amaranthin via CqAmaSy.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream of betanidin; immediate precursor step to amaranthin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates betanidin to produce betanin; used to show that betanin is the substrate for CqAmaSy1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: When substituted for CqCDOPA5GT with CqCYP76AD1-1, CqDODA-1 and CqAmaSy1, enabled amaranthin formation, proving betanin is the substrate of CqAmaSy1.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Confirmed in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves by transient expression context.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pigment with nutraceutical and pharmaceutical interest.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration assay in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparator transient co-expression assay with HPLC analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DbB5GT was used as a specific betanin-forming enzyme in the transient assay. 2. Its use with CqAmaSy1 led to amaranthin production, demonstrating that betanin is the substrate of CqAmaSy1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful tool enzyme for pathway engineering and mechanistic validation of downstream amaranthin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an experimentally central pathway gene although derived from another species and used primarily for substrate determination.
